content.problemStatement:
For a permutation PPP of size NNN, and an integer k (1≤k≤N)k \ (1 \le k \le N)k (1≤k≤N), define f(P,k)f(P, k)f(P,k) as ∑i=1N−k+1max⁡(Pi,Pi+1,Pi+2,…,Pi+k−1)\displaystyle{\sum_{i = 1}^{N - k + 1} \max(P_i, P_{i + 1}, P_{i + 2}, \ldots, P_{i + k - 1})}i=1∑N−k+1​max(Pi​,Pi+1​,Pi+2​,…,Pi+k−1​), i.e. the sum of maximum elements in all kkk - sized subarrays.

Let g(N,k)g(N, k)g(N,k) denote the number of permutations PPP of size NNN which have the maximum value of f(P,k)f(P, k)f(P,k).

Given an integer NNN, find the value of g(N,k)g(N, k)g(N,k) for each 1≤k≤N1\le k \le N1≤k≤N.

Since the value can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Note that a permutation of size NNN consists of all elements from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN - the size of the permutation.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space-separated integers denoting the value of g(N,k)g(N, k)g(N,k) for each 1≤k≤N1\le k \le N1≤k≤N, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one possible permutation of size 111. Thus, f([1],1)=1f([1], 1) = 1f([1],1)=1 and g(1,1)=1g(1, 1) = 1g(1,1)=1.
Test case 222:

g(2,1)=2g(2, 1) = 2g(2,1)=2 as there are two permutations with maximum f(P,1)f(P, 1)f(P,1)

f([1,2],1)=1+2=3f([1,2],1) = 1+2 = 3f([1,2],1)=1+2=3
f([2,1],1)=2+1=3f([2,1],1) = 2+1 = 3f([2,1],1)=2+1=3


g(2,2)=2g(2, 2) = 2g(2,2)=2 as there are two permutations with maximum f(P,2)f(P, 2)f(P,2)

f([1,2],2)=2f([1,2],2) = 2f([1,2],2)=2
f([2,1],2)=2f([2,1],2) = 2f([2,1],2)=2



Test case 333: For k=1k = 1k=1 and k=3k = 3k=3, all permutations have the same value of f(P,k)f(P, k)f(P,k), thus the answer is 666. However, for k=2k = 2k=2, the optimal permutations are [2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1] and [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] only.
